diagram_quality.notes: The diagram is too cluttered with 13 nodes and 30+ connections. Major issues: (1) Circular dependencies create visual confusion (Core <-> Application Entry, Core <-> Routing, Routing <-> Debugging), (2) Examples and Documentation shouldn't be in an architecture diagram - they're not architectural components, (3) Too many bidirectional arrows make it hard to understand flow, (4) Color coding is underutilized (too many gray nodes).

suggestions: Remove examples and documentation from the architecture diagram - focus only on the core Flask framework components (would reduce from 13 to 8 nodes)
Eliminate or clarify circular dependencies - the bidirectional arrows between Core, Application Entry, Routing, and Debugging suggest architectural issues or misrepresentation
Establish a clearer hierarchy: Application Entry at top → Core Framework → specialized components (Routing, Request/Response, Templates, etc.) below
Reduce the number of edges by showing only primary dependencies, not every possible interaction
Better utilize color coding: use one color for infrastructure (blue), one for runtime components (green), one for support utilities (gray)
Consider creating two separate diagrams: (1) Core Architecture (6-7 nodes) and (2) Examples & Extensions
